stories.headline: US rescues second fighter jet airman shot down over Iran
stories.summary: Trump warns Tehran ‘you’ll be living in hell’ unless the Strait of Hormuz is opened by Tuesday

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump vows to hit power plants, bridges over Hormuz closure
stories.summary: Tehran says shot down two US C-130 planes, two helicopters, as Iran's infrastructure attacks cause fires across Gulf.

stories.headline: What we know so far about rescue of US airman in Iran
stories.summary: The US has rescued the missing crew member of the US F-15 fighter jet which was shot down over southern Iran.

stories.headline: Iran allows Iraqi ships through Hormuz strait
stories.summary: Tehran’s decision could allow 3mn b/d of oil to be released to international markets

stories.headline: US Successfully Rescues Second Airman from Iran
stories.summary: Breaking News on Bloomberg This Weekend - there has been a successful rescue of a second US airman from Iran. Bloomberg's White House correspondent Jeff Mason and Bloomberg's Jerusalem reporter Dan Williams join hosts Christina Ruffini and Joe Mathieu and provide insight on the context and on going situation in Iran. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Rescues Missing Airman as Iran Strikes Gulf Arab States
stories.summary: US forces rescued an airman from Iran days after his fighter jet was shot down, while the Islamic Republic’s continued attacks sparked fires that damaged Kuwait’s oil headquarters and shut down an Emirati petrochemicals plant.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy warns US-Iran war could divert critical aid from Ukraine
stories.summary: President Zelenskyy has warned that a prolonged US-Israeli war with Iran is threatening the supply of vital Patriot missiles and boosting Russia’s economy through surging oil prices.

stories.headline: Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
stories.summary: Tehran says Iraq will face no restrictions in waterway, praising country's 'struggle' against the US.

stories.headline: Countries must not hoard fuel during Iran war, warns IEA
stories.summary: Fatih Birol makes veiled reference to China as he urges nations to avoid export bans despite worsening supply shock

stories.headline: Oil Tanker Carrying Iraqi Cargo Seen Transiting Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: An oil tanker carrying crude from Iraq appeared to be transiting the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint, a day after Iran said its neighbor had special exemption to use the waterway.

stories.headline: Uproar in Germany over law requiring men get military approval for long stays abroad
stories.summary: Ministry clarifies clause affecting those up to age 45 that is part of legislation that came into effect in JanuaryA little-noticed clause in sweeping changes to Germany’s military service policy has caused uproar after it emerged that the law requires men aged up to 45 to get permission from the armed forces before any significant stay abroad, even in peacetime.The legislation, which went into effect on 1 January, aims to bolster the military an

stories.headline: Nearly 100 NSW service stations fined $1,100 each over misleading petrol prices amid fuel shortage crackdown
stories.summary: Most of the stations hit with penalty infringement notices were in regional NSW, while 23 were in SydneyGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastNinety-three service stations in New South Wales have been issued on-the-spot fines for misrepresenting their prices amid Australia’s fuel crisis – although none are facing penalties for price gouging.A two-week compliance blitz has seen inspectors visit about 75% – or just under 1,800

stories.headline: Editor's Choice: Energy crisis forces Asia into COVID-like restrictions
Cambodia unveils statue to honour famous landmine-sniffing rat
stories.summary: The statue honours the late African giant pouched rat who sniffed out more than 100 landmines during his lifetime.

stories.headline: Renters’ Rights Act brings big changes to UK property market
stories.summary: New rules aim to provide safety and security for tenants, but landlords are anxious

stories.headline: Campaign to curb cars in Berlin sparks uproar ahead of election
stories.summary: Conservatives and far right are opposing a citizens’ initiative to restrict traffic in the German capital

stories.headline: UK courts Anthropic to expand in London after US defence clash
stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s government steps up efforts to get American AI start-up to grow its presence in Britain

stories.headline: The Potential Economic Impact of Ending Birthright Citizenship
stories.summary: The Supreme Court heard oral arguments in Trump v. Barbara to consider whether President Trump's executive order restricting birthright citizenship was constitutional. Princeton University Center for Migration & Development Research Fellow Phillip Connor and University of Notre Dame Keough School of Global Affairs Professor Francesc Ortega join Joe Mathieu and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the economic impact the move cou

stories.headline: Danantara Advances Asset Manager Merger With $159 Million Deal
stories.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund is advancing a plan to combine the asset management units of state-owned lenders to boost their regional competitiveness.

stories.headline: Why McDonald’s and KFC are growing like wildfire in China
stories.summary: Western fast-food brands are expanding into the countryside

stories.headline: More Drivers Are Showing an Interest in Electric Vehicles
stories.summary: From the gas pump to the auto show, drivers are talking about electric vehicles.

stories.headline: Stephen Miller Is Still Pursuing His Immigration Agenda, but More Quietly
stories.summary: The architect of President Trump’s mass deportation campaign wants “a moratorium on immigration from third world countries until we can heal ourselves as a nation.” The chaos in Minneapolis has not pushed him off that course.

stories.headline: Japan's corporate bond issuance hits record high on retail investors
Min Aung Hlaing elected Myanmar president despite international criticism
State medical insurance reform aims to expand access to healthcare
stories.summary: The reform will introduce digital referrals, a national health insurance fund and a guaranteed package of medical services funded through the state budget. Officials say the changes aim to improve efficiency, expand access and reduce informal payments.

stories.headline: Explosives found near gas pipeline connecting Serbia and Hungary, Vučić says
stories.summary: Serbian president Aleksandar Vučić has said that explosives were found by police near a pipeline that carries gas from Russia to Serbia and Hungary, but did not elaborate on potential suspects or their motives.

stories.headline: Help or hinderance? Amateur archaeologist digs at German castle ruins without permission
stories.summary: Germany's cultural heritage community is embroiled in a row after an amateur archaeologist uncovered an ancient construction. The problem however is that he didn't have permission to dig at the site of Metilstein Castle near Eisenach.

stories.headline: Leipzig Eierlei festival celebrates the 'eggstraordinary' art of Easter decorations
stories.summary: For some painting eggs is an integral part of Easter, but exhibitors at the Eierlei Festival in Leipzig take this tradition to a new level as craft and art experts show what can be done with supposedly "simple" decorations.

stories.headline: What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces are searching for a missing American crew member after a US warplane was shot down.

stories.headline: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
stories.summary: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.

stories.headline: US and Iran race to find missing downed pilot as Lebanon faces intense Israeli strikes
stories.summary: The Israeli military have relaunched evacuation orders from the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, warning of more strikes, claiming they are targeting Hezbollah. Over 1.2 million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the beginning of the war.

stories.headline: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
stories.summary: Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached.

stories.headline: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.headline: New German conscription law: eligible men may now need approval for trips abroad
stories.summary: Men aged 17 to 45 may now need approval from the Bundeswehr for trips abroad lasting more than three months. What is behind the new military service law?

stories.headline: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
stories.summary: The snap was taken aboard the Orion capsule by its commander, Reid Wiseman, as the crew head towards the Moon.

stories.headline: Pointed News Quiz | Resignations, Wearable, Food
stories.summary: Christina Ruffini, Joe Mathieu and Lisa Mateo play Pointed on “Bloomberg This Weekend”! Wager your points, leverage your bets and answer wisely. A new quiz is available to play each week on Bloomberg.com (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump Directs Officials to Pay All D.H.S. Employees
stories.summary: The memorandum calls for paying employees at the Coast Guard, Federal Emergency Management Agency and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency who have gone without pay during a record-long shutdown.

stories.headline: Trump Contemplates Other Cabinet Changes as He Faces Political Clock
stories.summary: With the midterms approaching, the president may be running out of time to get new cabinet members confirmed without bipartisan support.

stories.headline: Trump files emergency appeal to keep building White House ballroom
stories.summary: “Time is of the essence!” Justice Department lawyers wrote, saying a ruling to pause construction puts the president at risk.

stories.headline: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
stories.summary: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

stories.headline: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
stories.summary: Cameroon's President Paul Biya, 93, won his eighth successive term last year in a disputed election.

stories.headline: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
stories.summary: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.

stories.headline: Pakistan Says It Will Repay Matured Loan Deposits to UAE
stories.summary: Pakistan will return matured loan deposits to the United Arab Emirates, in a move that will strain the South Asian nation’s reserves.

stories.headline: Sleijpen Says ECB Discussion Will Be Rate Hike or Hold: Podcast
stories.summary: The European Central Bank’s next discussion will between increasing interest rates or holding them steady, according to Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen.

stories.headline: Some Voters Say Congress Is Too Old. These Black Democrats Aren’t Leaving.
stories.summary: As older members of Congress head for the exits amid growing pressure for fresh faces in the Democratic Party, some of the most seasoned Black lawmakers are resisting retirement.

stories.headline: New Attorney General, Same Albatross: Trump’s Quest for Retribution
stories.summary: The name atop the Justice Department’s organizational chart matters less than the presence of a president whose demands for revenge have become so extreme that even his most obsequious appointees have fallen short.

stories.headline: Arrest of Wisconsin Mosque Leader Was Tied to Trump Antisemitism Campaign
stories.summary: Immigration lawyers and former federal officials say the case of Salah Sarsour echoes those against other pro-Palestinian activists.

stories.headline: Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
stories.summary: After Trump ousted Pam Bondi, her deputy is set to lead the department — and take on many of the same challenges.

stories.headline: Meloni in the Gulf: harmony on gas and defence with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The focus of the talks of Italian prime minister with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia was the restoration of energy supplies and security in Hormuz. Italy offered technical support for affected infrastructure and defence against Iranian raids.

stories.headline: Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
stories.summary: An F-15 fighter jet and an A-10 attack plane were lost to hostile fire. Two search-and-rescue helicopters also were hit, injuring the crews, before safely returning to their base, officials said.

stories.headline: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
stories.summary: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

stories.headline: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
stories.summary: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

stories.headline: Reeling from war in the Middle East, Trump reaches for a reset
stories.summary: The president is in a precarious political moment seven months before the midterm elections, facing some of his lowest approval ratings and presiding over an unpopular war.

stories.headline: A day in the life of Asia’s fuel crisis
stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.headline: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

stories.headline: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but deny security breach
stories.summary: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

stories.headline: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.summary: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.headline: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.headline: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.summary: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warningOne person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves.The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 earthquake hits off Indonesia, killing one
stories.summary: The quake struck between the islands of Manado and Ternate. Tsunami warnings have now been lifted.

stories.headline: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
stories.summary: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

stories.headline: What to know about Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche
stories.summary: The former prosecutor has a long-standing relationship with President Trump, having represented him in the criminal hush-money case.

stories.headline: Macron calls Trump's remarks on his marriage 'inelegant'
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron has said Donald Trump's comments about his marriage were "neither elegant nor up to standard".

stories.headline: Alleged Bondi gunman loses court bid to suppress names of his family
stories.summary: Lawyers for Naveed Akram had argued his mother, brother and sister live in "constant fear".

stories.headline: Asia ramps up use of dirty fuels to cover energy shortfall triggered by Iran war
stories.summary: South Korea will delay the shutdown of coal-fired plants, while the Philippines also plans to boost the output of its coal-burning plantsGovernments across Asia are ramping up their use of coal, the dirtiest fossil fuel, as they try to cover huge energy shortfalls triggered by the US-Israel war on Iran.The move has triggered warnings from climate experts who point to coal’s devastating environmental impact, and say the energy crisis should be a w

stories.headline: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.summary: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.headline: Divorced couples in Japan can now share custody of their children
stories.summary: Japan was, until recently, the only G7 country to not recognise the legal concept of joint custody.

stories.headline: Rescuers abandon hope for whale stranded off German island
stories.summary: The whale became stranded on a sandbank before it was rescued, only to swim into shallow waters further along the coast.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms drench UAE and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Abnormally strong jet stream triggers deluge in Middle East, while north Africa braces for 60-80mph gustsAn unusual weather pattern unleashed severe thunderstorms across parts of the Middle East last week, battering countries including the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia. The Arabian peninsula – typically dominated by arid desert climates – received up to 150mm of rain in just a few days.The deluge was caused by an abnormally strong jet str

stories.headline: The plan to make IPOs great again
stories.summary: America’s regulators and market operators are teaming up to rekindle public listings
